ddls:
CREATE TABLE "competitions" (
  "competition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each competition */,
  "competition_name" TEXT /* Name of the competition */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the competition took place */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the competition took place */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the competition */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of competition (e.g., World Cup, IWAS World Games) */,
  "venue" TEXT /* Specific venue or stadium where the competition took place */,
  "weather_conditions" TEXT /* Weather conditions during the competition */,
  "audience_size" INTEGER /* Number of spectators present */,
  "organizer" TEXT /* Name of the organization hosting the competition */,
  "sponsors" TEXT /* List of sponsors (comma-separated string) */,
  "description" TEXT /* A brief description of the competition */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("competition_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each result */,
  "athlete_id" INTEGER /* ID of the athlete */,
  "competition_id" INTEGER /* ID of the competition */,
  "rank" INTEGER /* Rank of the athlete in the competition */,
  "points" INTEGER /* Points scored by the athlete in the competition */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the specific event */,
  "event_date" TEXT /* Date of the specific event */,
  "time_or_score" TEXT /* Time or score achieved by the athlete */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for time or score */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or comments about the result */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_results_athlete_id FOREIGN KEY ("athlete_id") REFERENCES athletes ("athlete_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_results_competition_id FOREIGN KEY ("competition_id") REFERENCES competitions ("competition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_results_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES events ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "athletes" (
  "athlete_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each athlete */,
  "athlete_name" TEXT /* Full name of the athlete */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country the athlete represents */,
  "sport" TEXT /* Sport the athlete competes in */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the athlete */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the athlete */,
  "height" REAL /* Height of the athlete in meters */,
  "weight" REAL /* Weight of the athlete in kilograms */,
  "coach_id" INTEGER /* ID of the athlete's coach */,
  "team" TEXT /* Name of the team the athlete is part of */,
  "injuries" TEXT /* History of injuries (text field) */,
  "achievements" TEXT /* Notable achievements or awards (text field) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("athlete_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_athletes_coach_id FOREIGN KEY ("coach_id") REFERENCES coaches ("coach_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, coach, athlete, spectator) */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the user (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the competition results */,
  "competition_id" INTEGER /* ID of the competition being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the competition results were accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device used (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_competition_id FOREIGN KEY ("competition_id") REFERENCES competitions ("competition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "coaches" (
  "coach_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each coach */,
  "coach_name" TEXT /* Full name of the coach */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the coach */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number */,
  "specialization" TEXT /* Sport or discipline the coach specializes in */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("coach_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sponsors" (
  "sponsor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sponsor */,
  "sponsor_name" TEXT /* Name of the sponsor */,
  "logo_url" TEXT /* URL to the sponsor's logo */,
  "contact_person" TEXT /* Name of the contact person at the sponsor */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the contact person */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sponsor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event */,
  "event_name" TEXT /* Name of the event */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the event */,
  "rules" TEXT /* Rules and regulations for the event */,
  "scoring_method" TEXT /* Method used to score the event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "injuries" (
  "injury_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each injury */,
  "athlete_id" INTEGER /* ID of the athlete */,
  "injury_date" TEXT /* Date of the injury */,
  "injury_type" TEXT /* Type of injury */,
  "recovery_status" TEXT /* Current status of recovery (e.g., recovering, recovered) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the injury */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("injury_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_injuries_athlete_id FOREIGN KEY ("athlete_id") REFERENCES athletes ("athlete_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "achievements" (
  "achievement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each achievement */,
  "athlete_id" INTEGER /* ID of the athlete */,
  "achievement_date" TEXT /* Date of the achievement */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the achievement */,
  "award" TEXT /* Name of the award received */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the achievement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("achievement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_achievements_athlete_id FOREIGN KEY ("athlete_id") REFERENCES athletes ("athlete_id")
)
